content:
If you've ever hacked solo because you couldn't find teammates in time, or ended up in a mismatched team because Discord servers move too fast to actually vet anyone — this one's for you.

I've been building HackerMate, a full-stack platform designed to fix the two most painful parts of hackathons:

• Before the hackathon: finding the right team
Instead of scrolling through chaotic Discord/WhatsApp threads, HackerMate lets you:

Browse and create teams

Search for teammates by skill, role, or college

Apply to join a team or invite people directly

• During the hackathon: actually staying organized
Once your team is formed, HackerMate becomes your mini project OS:

Task management

Daily standups

A shared project board

The stack:

Next.js (App Router)

Supabase for auth + database

Tailwind CSS with a custom dark design system

TypeScript throughout

It started life as "HackOS" before I rebranded, and it's now live at hackermate.in. Right now it has ~35 early users, mostly friends and people from my network — which has been super useful for feedback, but I'm now trying to get real hackathon teams using it in the wild.

If you're participating in any upcoming hackathons, I'd genuinely love for you to try forming a team on HackerMate and tell me what breaks. Feedback — good or brutal — welcome in the comments.

What's the most annoying part of finding a hackathon team for you?
